title: save_key_package_capped: device at cap cannot refresh its own key package
description: save_key_package_capped counts all rows for pub_id and returns Ok(false) if count >= MAX_KEY_PACKAGES_PER_USER (10). But if the device already has a row at (pub_id, device_id), the subsequent INSERT OR REPLACE would be an UPDATE, not an INSERT — the count would not increase. A legitimate device that rotates its key package when the per-user cap is already full (e.g. 10 distinct devices) gets RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED even though its update would not exceed the limit. Trigger: 10 devices publish; one of them calls PUBLISH_KEY_PACKAGE again to refresh; it is incorrectly rejected.
